skill.name: scenario-testing
skill.description: Guides agents to validate features with scenario-driven tests that exercise real dependencies and avoid mocks.
skill.summary: Creates practical scenario tests for feature validation with real systems.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks and ordinary testing language. A medium risk remains because the skill explicitly encourages use of real external services and asking users for API credentials.

medium_findings.title: Real External Service and Credential Use Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use actual external APIs, real databases, and real auth services, and to ask users for credentials when needed. This can expose secrets or affect live systems if users do not isolate test accounts and sandbox services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly require real services and credential acquisition. There is no evidence of exfiltration, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives. The cited text uses markdown inline code for file names, directories, and checklist items, with no executable shell instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain markdown formatting only. No Ruby code, shell command, command substitution, or user-controlled execution path appears in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Testing Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines describe the skill and scenario fields, and they do not name or invoke cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive testing language and a list of scenario specification fields. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography exists.

low_findings.title: Testing Headings Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are false positives. The cited lines are headings and bullet text about invalid scenarios and common testing violations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines do not request host, process, network, or environment enumeration. They only categorize testing practices.

content.value_statement: Many generated tests pass while missing real integration failures. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create scenario tests with real dependencies.
actual_capabilities: Directs agents to write scenario tests in a .scratch directory.
Requires tests to exercise real dependencies instead of mocks.
Promotes reusable scenario patterns into scenarios.jsonl documentation.
Defines completion criteria for feature validation.
Identifies testing practices that make scenario results unreliable.
limitations: It does not provide executable test runners or framework-specific libraries.
It may require sandbox credentials or test service accounts.
It can be slow when scenarios depend on real external systems.
It needs human review before scenarios touch production services.

use_cases.title: Validate a New Feature
use_cases.description: Create a scenario that proves the feature works through the real application flow.

use_cases.title: Improve Integration Coverage
use_cases.description: Replace fragile mock-based checks with scenarios that exercise real storage and services.

use_cases.title: Document Reusable Test Patterns
use_cases.description: Convert passing scenario patterns into durable specifications for future regression testing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Scenario
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one feature validation path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a scenario test for this feature in .scratch. Use real dependencies where safe, avoid mocks, and explain the expected pass condition.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Bug Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request after a defect is repaired.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scenario that reproduces the fixed bug and proves the repair works. Keep it independent from other scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Coverage to Scenarios
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for replacing mock-heavy tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these existing tests and propose scenario tests that use real storage, real service boundaries, and clear given-when-then outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Scenario Suite
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for reusable validation design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a scenario suite for this workflow. Include independent scenarios, required sandbox dependencies, and patterns to promote into scenarios.jsonl.

output_examples.output: A scenario plan that creates a test user, calls the real registration flow, verifies persisted state, and records the pass criteria.

output_examples.input: Turn this payment bug fix into a scenario.
output_examples.output: A focused validation path using a sandbox payment account, expected failure prevention, and cleanup steps for repeatable execution.

output_examples.input: Document the scenario pattern after it passes.
output_examples.output: A concise scenario specification with name, given state, user action, expected result, and validated behavior.

best_practices: Use sandbox services and dedicated test accounts for all external dependencies.
Keep each scenario independent, repeatable, and safe to run in parallel.
Promote only stable, useful scenario patterns into committed documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not use mocks to claim that a feature is validated.
Do not run scenarios against production systems without explicit approval.
Do not commit scratch files or credentials created during validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help test?
faq.answer: It helps validate features through scenario tests that exercise real application behavior and real dependencies.

faq.question: Does it ban all unit tests?
faq.answer: It treats unit tests as secondary. Feature completion depends on passing scenario tests.

faq.question: Where should temporary scenarios go?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to place temporary scenario files in .scratch and keep that directory ignored.

faq.question: Can it use external APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, but use sandbox or test mode services. Avoid production systems unless a human explicitly approves.

faq.question: Does it generate committed test specifications?
faq.answer: It can promote recurring scenario patterns into scenarios.jsonl as documented specifications.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is handling real credentials and external services safely during scenario validation.
